steps.title: Find BC using the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Triangle ABC has a right angle at C, meaning BC ⊥ AC. With AB = √61 (hypotenuse) and AC = 6 (one leg), apply the Pythagorean theorem:
$$BC^2 + 6^2 = (\sqrt{61})^2$$
$$BC^2 + 36 = 61$$
$$BC^2 = 25$$
$$BC = 5$$

steps.title: Establish similar triangles ABC and AED
steps.content: Both BC and ED are perpendicular to the line containing A, C, and E, so BC ∥ ED. Triangle ABC has a right angle at C, and triangle AED has a right angle at E. Both triangles share angle ∠A, so by AA similarity, triangles ABC and AED are similar.

steps.title: Apply the similarity ratio to find the area
steps.content:
The scale factor from ABC to AED is:
$$k = \frac{AE}{AC} = \frac{6 + 18}{6} = \frac{24}{6} = 4$$
The height of triangle ADE scales by this factor:
$$ED = 4 \cdot BC = 4 \cdot 5 = 20$$
Therefore, the area is:
$$\frac{1}{2} \cdot 24 \cdot 20 = 240 \text{ square units}$$
